title: artemisia vulgaris var. discolor
intro: Artemisia vulgaris var. discolor, also known as sweet wormwood or yellow mugwort, is a variant of the common wormwood plant, characterized by its yellow flowers and distinctive appearance. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to repel insects and its antimalarial and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of sweet wormwood include its potential to alleviate symptoms of malaria, reduce fever, and support digestive health. Artemisia vulgaris var. discolor can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, allowing for a range of uses in natural remedies and traditional medicine.
